idea: promote LHS tensor map to L2_128B
justification: small square-like shape with low occupancy can benefit from caching the compact lhs descriptor/tile stream

idea: promote LHS and metadata tensor maps to L2_256B
justification: occupancy-limited tiny-M/N shapes may hide less latency, so cache-bias the lhs and metadata descriptors

idea: add __launch_bounds__(256,1) to reduce register pressure assumptions
justification: occupancy-limited behavior suggests testing compiler residency guidance

idea: promote both RHS tensor maps to L2_128B
justification: with N=256 the rhs panel footprint is compact enough that L2 reuse may help

idea: tighten warpgroup wait depth from 1 to 0
justification: latency-sensitive low-occupancy kernels sometimes benefit from more aggressive consumer progress

idea: insert producer nanosleep(100) after full-stage barrier arrival
justification: brief producer pacing can smooth issue pressure when only a few warpgroups are resident

idea: port the 4096-square double-nanosleep structure and switch WARP_N to 128
justification: the nearby small-square family favored extra pacing; test that motif with a narrower warp-N decomposition

idea: promote output tensor map to L2_256B
justification: small output tiles may benefit from stronger write-side locality hints

idea: relax warpgroup wait depth from 1 to 2
justification: test whether deeper overlap offsets the low-residency stall pattern

idea: insert producer nanosleep(100) after metadata barrier arrival
justification: metadata arrival pacing may reduce synchronization imbalance on this tiny shape

idea: remove output shared-memory padding
justification: for N=256 the bank-conflict tradeoff may differ from the reference shape

idea: promote metadata tensor map to L2_256B only
justification: metadata is tiny and especially reusable across the one-column-N regime

idea: switch WARP_N to 128 and add only the full-stage 100ns nanosleep
justification: decompose the square-port win into narrower consumer tiling plus lighter producer pacing

idea: increase output shared-memory padding to 16
justification: alternate smem stride can change store bank mapping for the small output tile

idea: insert producer nanosleep(200) after full-stage barrier arrival
justification: a stronger pacing delay may better absorb synchronization latency

idea: promote all tensor maps to L2_256B
justification: the overall working set is tiny enough to test a maximal cache-bias policy

idea: switch WARP_N to 128 and add only the metadata 100ns nanosleep
justification: tests whether the narrower warp-N plus early producer pacing is the transferable part of the small-shape motif

idea: add __launch_bounds__(384,1) to constrain compiler occupancy assumptions
justification: try a stronger launch-bound hint while keeping one-block residency legal

idea: insert producer nanosleep(200) after metadata barrier arrival
justification: a longer post-metadata pause may better line up subsequent TMA issue for the small kernel

idea: promote only the LHS tensor map to L2_256B
justification: bias just the lhs stream more aggressively without touching rhs/output policy

idea: increase output shared-memory padding to 32
justification: a wider stride provides another distinct bank-mapping experiment

idea: promote both RHS tensor maps to L2_256B
justification: rhs panel reuse may respond to stronger promotion with only one output tile along N

idea: switch WARP_N to 128 and tighten wait depth to 0
justification: combine narrower consumer decomposition with more aggressive overlap to probe a different scheduling regime

idea: add __launch_bounds__(320,1) as an intermediate residency hint
justification: probe whether a milder launch-bound constraint changes register allocation more favorably
